topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The accusation landed in the lecture hall like a physical blow. Every head in the room seemed to swivel, a collective, silent judgment settling over me. My hands, gripping the edge of my desk, began to tremble almost imperceptibly. I could feel the heat rising in my chest, a tight, constricting pressure that made each breath shallow. When the professor finally addressed me, his voice was level, clinical, and utterly devoid of warmth. I stared at the floor tiles, focusing intensely on the grout lines, desperately trying to anchor myself against the sudden, dizzying sense of being utterly misunderstood and invalidated.

[story 2]

"This is unacceptable," she stated, her voice sharp enough to cut glass. I watched her face, noting the rigid set of her jaw and the way her eyes narrowed. A strange, cold numbness started to spread through my limbs, making my movements sluggish. I opened my mouth to offer a defense, but the words caught in my throat, thick and unformed. I just stood there, my spine feeling suddenly rigid, wishing the floor would simply swallow me whole. The silence stretching between us felt immense, amplifying the sound of my own frantic pulse drumming against my ribs.

[story 3]

I re-read the email three times, the words blurring on the screen. Each sentence felt like a deliberate slight, meticulously constructed to dismantle my work. A slow, burning sensation began to bloom behind my eyes, making it difficult to focus on anything external. When I finally gathered the courage to speak in the meeting, my voice emerged as a thin, reedy sound. I kept my gaze fixed just above the other people's heads, my shoulders drawn inward, a physical manifestation of a deep, internal withdrawal. I could feel the urge to simply dissolve into the air, to become invisible.
